title: Typography
summary: Typography plays a crucial role in shaping the look and feel of our design. It not only impacts readability but also establish our brand identity.

columns.text: Guidelines contains how to use font styles, sizes, and weights effectively across different elements, from headings and body text to small UI details.
Font weight is an important tool in design that helps establish hierarchy, create emphasis, and improve readability of your design.
GDS – SEB SanSerif offers the weights bold, medium, the new weight book, regular and light. Our styles are using the middle spectra. Bold and light can be used in special cases.
Our type scale ranges from 64px down to 12px, with each size serving a specific role in structuring information effectively.

Hierarchy is shown through changes in font weight, size and colors. The type scale categorizes styles into roles. The type styles are defined by five roles – display, headline, preamble, body and detail.

columns.content:
_Tone: Expressive_  
Used for large, expressive text that highlights key messages and creates strong visual impact. Typically used in hero sections, landing pages, and promotional content. Display text is perfect when you need to raise the volume.

columns.title: Usage
columns.content:
*   Hero sections and landing pages
    
*   Editorial or marketing content
    
*   Promotional cards and banners

columns.title: Guidelines
columns.content:
*   Use sparingly to maintain impact
    
*   Avoid using multiple display styles within the same view

_Tone: Structural_  
Used for structuring content and establishing a clear hierarchy across a page. Suitable for page titles, section headings, and titles in cards or content sections.

columns.title: Usage
columns.content:
*   Page titles and section headings
    
*   Structuring content within layouts
    
*   Titles in cards and content sections

columns.title: Guidelines
columns.content:
*   Use heading levels consistently to maintain hierarchy
    
*   Avoid skipping heading levels when structuring content

_Tone: Introductory_  
Used for introductory text placed below a display or heading to provide context or summarize the content that follows. The preamble is generally larger than body text but smaller than headings.

columns.title: Usage
columns.content:
*   Introducing pages or sections
    
*   Summarizing articles or content areas
    
*   Providing context before body text

columns.title: Guidelines
columns.content:
*   Keep preambles concise
    
*   Use together with headings or display styles

_Tone: Readable_  
Used for running text and longer passages of content such as paragraphs, articles, and descriptions.

columns.title: Usage
columns.content:
*   Paragraphs and descriptions
    
*   Articles and informational content
    
*   Text-heavy layouts

columns.title: Guidelines
columns.content:
*   Use consistent spacing between paragraphs
    
*   Prioritize readability over visual emphasis

_Tone: Functional_  
Used for small UI text such as labels, metadata, helper text, and component text. Can also be used as an overline above headings or display text.

columns.title: Usage
columns.content:
*   Labels and metadata
    
*   Helper text and status information
    
*   Text in buttons, inputs, and tooltips
    
*   Overlines above headings or display text

columns.title: Guidlines
columns.content:
*   Keep detail text short and functional
    
*   Avoid using detail styles for long-form content

Pairing the different text styles is key to creating a strong visual hierarchy. _These are defined with a few examples but a more cohesive and thorough guide will be available soon._

Equally important is proper spacing between these elements. It helps separate and define relationships between text styles, and ensures a natural reading rhythm.
